tagline: Your bracket is too safe. This one isn't.

round_of_64.reasoning: Auburn's interior dominance is too much even in a chaos bracket. The 1-16 game is the one chalky pick I'll give you. Everything after this is a bloodbath.
Louisville's half-court offense is built for comfort, not chaos. Creighton's motion scheme creates the exact spacing problems Louisville's defense was never designed to handle.
Michigan's defense has given up 12-seed-caliber offenses all season and never fixed it. UC San Diego is 24-11 against the spread in single-elimination formats. Nobody is watching film on them.
Yale's precision offense is real but Texas A&M's physicality is tournament-caliber. I need Texas A&M to set up the UC San Diego upset in Round of 32.
Ole Miss hasn't won a tournament game in years and the metrics confirm it. North Carolina is a First-Four survivor with the chip on their shoulder that breeds upsets.
Iowa State wins this one. I need them in the second round to set up the chaos that follows. Even in a chaos bracket, someone has to be the setup man.
Marquette wins this one cleanly. The chaos in this region doesn't start here.
Michigan State advances. I need them as the sacrificial second-round opponent. Marquette beats them, which sets up Iowa State — but UC San Diego beats everyone.
Duke advances. I have them going down in the Sweet 16 but they need to get there first. No chaos in the 1-16 game.
Baylor's guard play and ball-screen offense is exactly what Mississippi State can't guard. The 8-9 game breaks toward the better half-court team, and that's Baylor.
Oregon advances. I need them to beat Duke in the Sweet 16, so they can't fall here. Some chaos requires setup.
Arizona advances. They're Oregon's Round of 32 opponent and that game goes to Oregon in my bracket.
BYU's defense was built for the Mountain West pace, not VCU's press. The Rams generate turnovers at a rate BYU's ball-handlers have never seen all season. Chaos starts here.
Wisconsin advances. Even chaos brackets need occasional chalk. Wisconsin sets up as Alabama's S16 matchup.
Saint Mary's slow-down game gets completely neutralized by Vanderbilt's guard pressure. The 10-seed has the athleticism edge and tournament motivation to upset the WCC system here.
Alabama advances. They're going to the Final Four in this bracket. The chaos in the East isn't Alabama falling — it's everyone else.
Houston wins this. The chaos for Houston comes in Round of 32. Even I won't pick a 16-seed.

Gonzaga's offense is elite but Georgia's defense was built for exactly this type of half-court grinder. The Bulldogs don't have the defensive identity to stop Georgia's disciplined SEC-hardened attack. 9-seed wins.
Clemson's offense stalled in three of their last five games and McNeese's defense feeds on exactly that. The Southland Conference champion is built for the pace Clemson creates. 12-seed alert.
Purdue advances. I need them for the Kentucky matchup later. The chaos in this region isn't in the first round.

Illinois's offense has been inconsistent all season and Xavier's Big East defense is specifically designed to punish that inconsistency. The play-in survivor mentality is real here — Xavier brings the energy.
Kentucky advances and they're going to the Final Four. The chaos isn't Kentucky losing early — it's Kentucky winning the whole region.
UCLA's offense relies on isolation plays that Utah State's pack-line defense specifically neutralizes. The Mountain West champion is built for exactly this type of physical 7-10 game.
Tennessee advances. They fall to Kentucky in the Sweet 16. The chaos isn't a 15-seed upset of Tennessee.
Florida advances and they fall in the Elite Eight. The chaos in the West builds slowly and explodes at exactly the right moment.
UConn's championship DNA doesn't save them here. Oklahoma's SEC guard play creates turnovers in transition that UConn's defense cannot recover from. The 9-seed wins.
Memphis's defense gave up 80+ points four times in February. Colorado State's efficient half-court offense is built to exploit teams that can't guard the perimeter. 12-seed alert.
Maryland advances. I need them to lose to Colorado State in the Round of 32. The chaos in the West builds through the bracket, not in this game.

Missouri's perimeter defense ranks 187th nationally. Drake's three-point shooting (38.4%) is built to exploit exactly that. Missouri Valley teams with elite shooting upset SEC 6-seeds at a 41% historical clip.
Texas Tech advances. They fall to Drake in the Round of 32. Even chaos brackets need legitimate opponents.
Kansas advances. They fall to St. John's in Round of 32. The chaos in the West isn't Kansas-Arkansas.
St. John's advances. They make the Sweet 16 and lose to Drake. Pitino's team is the respectability foil for the real chaos pick in this region.

round_of_32.reasoning: Auburn advances past Creighton. The Creighton upset of Louisville sets the table but Auburn's interior is a wall Creighton can't scale.

Texas A&M's half-court defense was built to contain SEC forwards. UC San Diego's system runs through three-ball creation and cutting — exactly the offense Texas A&M's defense hasn't seen all season. The 12-seed keeps rolling.
Iowa State's defense clamps North Carolina's transition game. Even in a chaos bracket, Iowa State advances here to set up as UC San Diego's Sweet 16 victim.
Michigan State advances past Marquette. They reach the S16 as UC San Diego's second big-seed victim. The narrative is building.
Duke handles Baylor. The chaos in the East isn't Duke falling in the second round. That comes in the Sweet 16 when Oregon ends the Cooper Flagg era early.
Oregon's athleticism neutralizes Arizona's perimeter game. Dana Altman's team is built for exactly the 4-5 matchup. Oregon advances to end Duke's run.
Wisconsin survives VCU's pressure through ball movement and deliberate pace. VCU's press requires pace; Wisconsin gives them the one thing that kills it.
Alabama handles Vanderbilt. The Tide are a Final Four team in this bracket. Vanderbilt was just the gateway upset victim.

Houston's offense is too dependent on half-court execution and Georgia's zone defense is specifically designed to disrupt that. The Cougars haven't faced a disciplined SEC-style 2-3 zone all season. Georgia's disciplined attack exposes Houston's one exploitable weakness.
Purdue's interior game is too much for McNeese. The Southland champion had its moment. Purdue advances to face Georgia in the Sweet 16.
Kentucky handles Xavier in the Round of 32. The Wildcats are going to the Final Four — this is their easiest game on the path.
Tennessee's defensive intensity overwhelms Utah State's system. Rick Barnes' team advances to the Sweet 16 where Kentucky ends the run.
Florida handles Oklahoma. The chaos in the West comes in the Elite Eight, not the second round. Florida needs to get to the E8 to be Drake's victim.

Maryland's offense stalled in five second-half situations against elite defenses this year. Colorado State's efficient attack and disciplined defense is exactly the profile that beats a 4-seed expecting an easy path to the Sweet 16.

Texas Tech's defense is built to clamp half-court offenses. Drake runs early offense at a rate that Texas Tech's defense has never faced at this tournament level. The Bulldogs' shooting exploits the seam.
St. John's Big East depth and Rick Pitino's adjustment game is too much for Kansas in a close game. The Jayhawks haven't faced this level of perimeter pressure consistently and it shows.

sweet_16.reasoning: Auburn's backcourt has been exposed in late-game half-court situations all season and UC San Diego's system lives in exactly that space. The 12-seed doesn't care about Johni Broome because they're not trying to post up. Nobody has a gameplan for this.
Iowa State's defense holds Michigan State under 60 points. Izzo's team thrives on physicality and Iowa State's switchable defense takes that away. The 3-seed advances to face UC San Diego.

Duke's offense relies on ball-screen creation and Oregon's long-armed defense disrupts exactly that. Cooper Flagg hasn't faced a defensive scheme like this all season and it shows in the second half. The chalk dies here.

Alabama's athleticism is a problem Wisconsin's methodical offense can't counter. The Tide's transition scoring comes in waves that Wisconsin's deliberate system can't match. Alabama reaches the Elite Eight.
Purdue's interior game is too much for Georgia even with the upset momentum. Purdue advances to face Kentucky in the Elite Eight.
Kentucky's talent level is the decisive factor against Tennessee in a physical Sweet 16. The Wildcats grind Tennessee down in the second half and advance to the Elite Eight.
Florida's length finally creates a matchup Colorado State can't solve. The run ends here. Florida advances to face Drake in the Elite Eight, where the real chaos happens.
St. John's size advantage should matter but Drake's three-point barrage is relentless. Pitino can't scheme away a 41% three-point shooting team. Drake reaches the Elite Eight.

elite_8.reasoning: Iowa State's half-court defense is methodical. UC San Diego's ball movement is too fast for that methodology. The 12-seed reaches the Final Four. Nobody has stopped this offense and Iowa State won't be the first.

Alabama's athleticism overwhelms Oregon's system at the Elite Eight level. The Tide's transition offense is unstoppable and Oregon doesn't have the depth to run with them for 40 minutes. Alabama reaches the Final Four.

Kentucky's talent level is the final variable that tips this game. Purdue's interior game is elite but Kentucky's athleticism and guard play opens up the court. The Wildcats advance to the Final Four.

Florida's length should win this game. But Drake's system doesn't care about Florida's length because they don't attack the paint. Three-point shooting at 41% versus Florida's perimeter defense at 201st nationally. The 11-seed does the unthinkable.

final_four.reasoning: Alabama versus Kentucky in the Final Four is the SEC rivalry game everyone wanted and nobody predicted at these seeds. Alabama's transition offense is the edge. Nate Oats out-executes in the second half.
The lowest-seeded Final Four matchup in modern tournament history. UC San Diego's ball movement is simply too good for Drake's help defense to account for. The 12-seed keeps its destiny alive.

championship.reasoning: Alabama's high-seed bias creates overconfidence. UC San Diego has beaten a 5-seed, a 4-seed, a 1-seed, a 3-seed, an 11-seed, and a 2-seed. Nobody has a defensive answer for their system. The 12-seed completes the greatest run in tournament history.
